title: athlete-guardian — trait composition
description: Two owned traits — `BelongsToAthleteGuardian` for downstream models that carry a `guardian_id` FK (Wave 4+ CustodyScheduleEntry + MedicalClearanceAuthorisation), `HasGuardianRelationships` on the User model exposing per-user guardianship accessors.

owned.description: Mixed into any downstream model that carries a `guardian_id` FK. Adds the `guardian()` belongsTo relation + a `resolveInGuardian(?AthleteGuardian)` query scope + a `booted()` hook that refuses to save when guardian.tenant_id != row.tenant_id (cross-tenant defense-in-depth).
usedBy: custody-schedule::CustodyScheduleEntry (Wave 4+ — pointing at the guardian who has custody for a given period)
medical-clearance::MedicalClearanceAuthorisation (Wave 4+ — pointing at the guardian who authorised medical care for an athlete session)

owned.description: Composed onto the User model. Adds sibling accessors: `guardianships()` (hasMany AthleteGuardian where user_id = this.id), `activeGuardianships()` (filtered by is_active), `primaryGuardianships()` (where is_primary=true), `authorisedForMedical()` (where has_legal_custody + can_authorise_medical_care). Read-only accessors; every write goes through AthleteGuardianProvisioner.
usedBy: user::User (exposes guardianships accessor to every consumer holding the User model)

athlete.reason: AthleteGuardian carries athlete_id — cascades tenant + branch through the athlete.

user.reason: AthleteGuardian carries user_id — enables .own scoping for guardians managing their own guardianships.

audit.reason: Every state mutation (verification, primary flip, role flags) is compliance-relevant.

athleteGuardian.notes: Order matters. BelongsToTenant precedes every scope-observing trait. BelongsToAthlete precedes BelongsToUser (athlete resolves the tenant-branch chain first). No BelongsToBranch — cascades through athlete. No BelongsToOrganization / BelongsToRegion — same reason.
